staticText: Use the Kenya Stamp Duty route only for an ordinary immovable-property transfer, gift inter vivos or lease under the Stamp Duty Act (Cap. 480), consolidated at 1 July 2025 and reviewed through 23 July 2026. For a sale, require both consideration and current open-market valuation and use the higher amount as the planning basis because section 10A permits government valuation and additional duty. For a gift, section 52 substitutes property value for consideration. The Schedule uses stepped shilling amounts: item 12A charges immovable property situated in a municipality at KSh 20 per KSh 500 through KSh 2,000 and then KSh 40 per KSh 1,000 or part; item 11 applies half those amounts outside a municipality. Do not infer municipality status from an informal urban or rural label; require the title, valuation or KRA assessment. For a lease, use the Lease Schedule rent bands: a definite term not over one year uses total rent payable; over one and not over three years uses average annual rent at the one-percent stepped band; another definite or indefinite term uses average annual rent at the two-percent stepped band. Under section 57, separately charge any premium or other non-rent consideration as a conveyance on sale. A perpetuity is outside the lease calculator because section 58 treats it as a conveyance on property value. Never use a flat 2% of property value as a lease proxy. Do not infer an exemption. The first-home provision concerns a first-time homeowner under an affordable housing scheme, not every first purchase and not a blanket KES 4 million exemption. KRA evidence for the specific instrument overrides the ordinary calculation. Section 6 generally provides 30 days from first execution or receipt in Kenya and 21 days after a Collector assessment notice, but do not calculate penalties or lateness. Exclude mortgages, partitions, powers of attorney, other deeds, legal fees, registration fees, CGT, valuation fees, payment confirmation and legal advice. No title number, KRA PIN, name, address or document is required.
